reforms.description: Chile increased public support for business R&D to raise business R&D from 0.1% of GDP to 0.3% of GDP, assuming a long-run elasticity of business R&D around 0.5.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D is expected to stimulate private sector investment in research, enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to increase public support for business R&D was announced in 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, which is crucial for enhancing the innovation capacity of the private sector and overall economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Increase public support for business R&D to raise business R&D from 0.1% of GDP to 0.3% of GDP.

reforms.description: A wide-ranging educational reform was introduced in 2015 to improve quality and equity from early childhood education and care to higher education, prohibiting practices that contribute to inequity and increasing teacher performance incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education quality enhances the skills of the workforce, which is essential for productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was introduced in 2015 and has been in effect since then.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the education system aimed at addressing long-standing inequities and improving overall educational outcomes, which is crucial for the country's economic development.
reforms.source_quote: A wide-ranging educational reform was introduced in 2015 to improve quality and equity from early childhood education and care (ECEC) to higher education.

reforms.description: The government created two public universities and 15 public VET centres, along with a higher education reform approved in January 2018 to address access and quality issues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding access to higher education and improving quality can enhance the skill level of the workforce, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The higher education reform was approved in January 2018 and has been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the higher education landscape in Chile, aiming to improve access and quality, which is vital for the country's economic future.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently created two public universities and 15 public VET centres, and a wide-ranging higher education reform, approved in January 2018, would address access and quality issues.

reforms.description: The 2012 reform of the R&D tax credit broadened its scope to internal expenditures, increased the annual tax ceiling, simplified administrative requirements, and eased domestic and international collaboration, significantly raising eligibility and take-up, especially for large firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the scope and accessibility of the R&D tax credit, the reform encourages more firms to invest in research and development, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has significantly raised the eligibility and take-up of the programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the way R&D is funded in Chile, enhancing the capacity for innovation across the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The reform of the 2008 R&D tax credit in 2012 broadened the scope of eligible R&D investments to internal expenditures, increased the annual tax ceiling, simplified administrative requirements and eased domestic and international collaborations.

reforms.description: Chile has developed significant efforts through its development agency, CORFO, to target promising sectors, particularly through direct R&D support, including a 2017 agreement with Albemarle for lithium production.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing R&D efforts on promising sectors, the initiative aims to enhance innovation and competitiveness in key industries, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The agency sealed an agreement in 2017 with Albemarle for the production of lithium.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a targeted approach to enhancing R&D in specific sectors, which is significant but not as broad as a national reform.
reforms.source_quote: Chile has developed significant efforts through its development agency, CORFO, to target promising sectors, in particular through direct R&D support.

reforms.description: Chile adopted a productivity and innovation agenda in 2014, focusing on promoting economic diversification, boosting high-growth sectors, and enhancing support for early-stage startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The agenda aims to enhance competitiveness and attract investments, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The agenda has been actively pursued since its adoption in 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The agenda represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing innovation and productivity across multiple sectors, indicating a significant structural change in policy.
reforms.source_quote: Chile adopted a productivity and innovation agenda in 2014 focusing on promoting the diversification of the economy, boosting sectors with high growth potential.

reforms.description: A new investment fund (Fondo de Inversión Estratégica - FIE) was established to manage projects in six strategic sectors, with public funding amounting to about USD 163 million over 2015-18.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to stimulate investment in strategic sectors, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been operational since its establishment in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant investment in targeted sectors, contributing to the overall innovation agenda.
reforms.source_quote: A new investment fund (Fondo de Inversión Estratégica - FIE) to manage projects in six strategic sectors was launched.

reforms.description: The 2014 structural reform agenda introduced 47 measures to boost competitiveness and productivity, including the establishment of a productivity commission to advise the government on competitiveness issues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the productivity commission aims to enhance competitiveness, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The productivity commission has been advising the government since its establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a dedicated body to assess and improve productivity represents a significant institutional change aimed at enhancing economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The 2014 structural reform agenda introduced 47 measures to boost competitiveness and productivity.

reforms.description: The new competition law adopted in 2016 strengthened the competition framework by introducing criminal sanctions for cartels and improving merger regimes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing competition, the law is expected to spur innovation and productivity among firms, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new competition law has been in effect since its adoption in 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major overhaul of the competition framework, which is crucial for fostering a competitive and innovative economy.
reforms.source_quote: Chile adopted a new competition law in 2016 which strengthened the competition framework.

reforms.description: The Chilean government has implemented an ambitious innovation plan for 2014-18, establishing new governance structures and funding mechanisms to enhance innovation capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a National Innovation Council and an Inter-ministerial Committee aims to streamline innovation efforts, potentially leading to increased productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have put forward an ambitious innovation plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how innovation is governed and funded in Chile, aiming to significantly enhance the country's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Acknowledging that innovation is a key pillar for the new economic model, the authorities have put forward an ambitious innovation plan in 2014-18.

reforms.description: Chile introduced a Smart Specialisation Strategy aimed at fostering public-private collaboration in strategic economic areas, supported by a public fund that finances initiatives to improve productivity and diversify the economy across six defined sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By targeting specific sectors for innovation and productivity improvements, the strategy is expected to enhance economic diversification and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The public fund has been operational since 2015, financing various initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how Chile approaches innovation policy, focusing on strategic sectors and fostering collaboration between public and private entities.
reforms.source_quote: Since 2015, a public fund (Fondo de Inversiones Estratégicas) also finances initiatives aimed at improving productivity and diversifying the economy in six sectors defined by the Government.

reforms.description: Chile's 2020 Digital Agenda aims to improve digital infrastructure and increase the use of ICTs, targeting broadband access for homes and schools, and promoting e-commerce among SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital infrastructure and ICT usage is expected to boost productivity and facilitate market access for firms, particularly SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The agenda includes measures to increase business innovation and productivity, especially for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Agenda represents a comprehensive strategy to enhance digital capabilities across the economy, which is crucial for competitiveness and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Chile’s 2020 Digital Agenda could improve digital infrastructure and the use of ICTs.

reforms.description: The Plan Nacional de Infraestructura de Telecomunicaciones (PNIT) aims to develop telecommunications infrastructure through public-private partnerships, ensuring open access.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving telecommunications infrastructure is expected to enhance connectivity, reduce costs, and foster innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan aims to develop the availability and quality of telecommunication infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: The PNIT represents a significant effort to enhance telecommunications infrastructure, which is vital for supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Plan Nacional de Infraestructura de Telecomunicaciones (PNIT) aims to develop the availability and quality of telecommunication infrastructure.

reforms.description: The recommendation includes expanding R&D support programmes that have proven effective while discontinuing or adjusting those that are inefficient, aiming to enhance the overall effectiveness of public investment in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reallocating resources to effective R&D programmes, the reform is expected to enhance innovation outcomes and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to expand and adjust R&D support programmes was made as part of the economic survey.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing R&D funding mechanisms, aiming to improve their efficiency and effectiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Expand R&D support programmes that are proven to work, and close down or adjust inefficient ones.

reforms.description: The proposal includes allowing refunds of R&D tax credits for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), which aims to enhance their capacity to invest in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to R&D tax credits, SMEs are incentivized to invest in innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Allow refunds of research and development (R&D) tax credits for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly targets SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Allow refunds of research and development (R&D) tax credits for SMEs.

reforms.description: The government introduced training programmes specifically designed for women from the poorest households to enhance their employability and skills, including transport and childcare subsidies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the skills and employability of women, the initiative aims to increase their participation in the labor market, which can enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The training programmes have been in place since 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative addresses a significant barrier to employment for women in vulnerable situations, aiming to improve their skills and job prospects.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced in 2014 training programmes (Más Capaz Mujer and Más Capaz Mujer Emprendedora) for women between 18 and 64 years old, belonging to the 60% poorest households.

reforms.description: In January 2018, the government passed a wide-ranging higher education reform aimed at addressing access and quality issues in tertiary education, creating a unified system for universities and vocational education and training (VET).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to improve the quality of higher education, which is essential for enhancing the skills of the workforce and boosting productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government passed the reform in January 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the higher education system, aiming to unify and improve quality across institutions, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In January 2018, the government passed a wide-ranging higher education reform, to address access and quality issues.
